8.reason: [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator), leading to potential out-of-bounds access when checking a[i+1]]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator). This may lead to reading beyond intended input when checking a[i+1] at i == n-1 or i == n. Specifically, when i == n, a[i] is '\0', and a[i+1] is out-of-bounds. Also, when i == 0 and a[0]=='-', a[i-1] accesses a[-1] 鈫 undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator), leading to potential out-of-bounds access when checking a[i+1]]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator) and a[n+1] (out-of-bounds) when a[i]=='-' at i==n. This causes undefined behavior and potential crash. Should be i < n.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator) and a[n+1] (out-of-bounds) when a[i]=='-' at i==n]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Loop runs to i == n, which accesses a[n] (null terminator), and when i == n and a[i]=='-', it will access a[n+1] (out-of-bounds) and a[n-1]; also, when i==0 and a[i]=='-', it accesses a[-1] (invalid)]

12.reason: [When i == 0, a[i-1] is a[-1] 鈫 undefined behavior; also when i == n-1, a[i+1] is a[n] (null byte), causing incorrect comparisons]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [No bounds check for i-1 and i+1; when i==0, i-1 is -1 (invalid), and when i==n-1, i+1 is n (valid only if n < array size, but a[n] is '\0'). Accessing a[-1] or comparing with '\0' can cause incorrect logic or crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [When i == 0, a[i-1] is a[-1] 鈫 undefined behavior; also when i == n-1, a[i+1] is a[n] (null byte), causing incorrect comparisons]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [When i==0, a[i-1] is a[-1] 鈫 out-of-bounds access. Similarly, when i==n-1, a[i+1] is a[n] (null terminator), which may be less than a[i-1], but also risks invalid comparison. No bounds check before accessing i-1 or i+1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [When i==0, a[i-1] is a[-1] (invalid); when i==n, a[i+1] is beyond string end 鈫 undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Accesses a[i-1] when i could be 0, leading to out-of-bounds read (undefined behavior); similarly, accesses a[i+1] when i could be n (end of string), also out-of-bounds]

14.reason: [Logic error: conditions mix lower/upper bounds incorrectly (e.g., a[i-1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z' allows 'a'-'Z'); also digits compared against integer 0/9 instead of character '0'/'9']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Logic error: conditions are malformed. Should check both ends belong to same category, e.g., (a[i-1]>='a' && a[i-1]<='z' && a[i+1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z'). Current condition allows mixed types like 'a' and 'Z'. Also, (a[i-1]>=0 && a[i+1]<=9) compares ASCII values: '0' is 48, so this condition is always true for printable chars since all are >0, and <=9 is false for digits (which are >=48). Should be (a[i-1]>='0' && a[i+1]<='9' && ...).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Logic error: conditions mix lower/upper bounds incorrectly (e.g., a[i-1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z' allows 'a'-'Z'); also digits compared against integer 0/9 instead of character '0'/'9']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Logic error: conditions like 'a[i-1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z'' do not ensure both are lowercase; e.g., a[i-1]='z', a[i+1]='a' would pass first part incorrectly. Also, 'a[i-1]>=0' compares char to int 0 (ASCII NUL), not '0'; should be 'a[i-1]>='0''.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect range checks: e.g., 'a[i-1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z'' doesn't ensure both are lowercase; also '0' vs 0 typo in digit check]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrect condition: e.g., 'a[i-1]>='a' && a[i+1]<='z'' does not ensure both are lowercase; also, 'a[i-1]>=0' compares char to integer 0, should be 'a[i-1]>='0''; same for upper bound]

27.reason: [b is not null-terminated. If the last character processed is not '\0', puts may print garbage. Need b[k]='\0'; before puts.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [b is not null-terminated, so puts may print garbage beyond intended output]
